nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.summary: An artificial intelligence company that has developed several AI models, including GPT-5.

nodes.name: GPT-5
nodes.summary: A large language model developed by OpenAI, requiring a mandatory enterprise license for API access.

nodes.name: Meta
nodes.summary: A technology company that has released three open-source models benchmarking within 8% of GPT-4 performance.

nodes.name: GPT-4
nodes.summary: A large language model developed by OpenAI, used as a benchmark for the performance of GPT-5.

nodes.name: Stanford
nodes.summary: A research university where researchers have published findings on the impact of OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: API
nodes.summary: A programming interface that provides access to OpenAI's models, requiring a mandatory enterprise license for GPT-5.

nodes.name: 60 days
nodes.summary: The time period given to existing customers to migrate to the new pricing model.

nodes.name: GPT-4 performance
nodes.summary: The performance benchmark used to compare the performance of GPT-5 and the open-source models from Meta.

nodes.name: Enterprise license
nodes.summary: A mandatory license required for API access to GPT-5, priced at $200 per month.

nodes.name: Pay-per-token model
nodes.summary: A pricing model for API access to OpenAI's models, discontinued for new customers.

nodes.name: Small startups
nodes.summary: Small companies that may be priced out of cutting-edge AI access due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Academic researchers
nodes.summary: Researchers from academic institutions that may be priced out of cutting-edge AI access due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Large corporations
nodes.summary: Large companies that may concentrate advanced AI capabilities due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Government agencies
nodes.summary: Government institutions that may concentrate advanced AI capabilities due to OpenAI's new pricing model.
